exercises.title: Nested if for Two-Factor Check
exercises.description: A nested if checks a second condition only when the outer condition passes. Implement a simple login check: first verify the username, then verify the password.
hints: The inner if only runs when the outer if is True.
Indentation shows which block each line belongs to.

exercises.title: Three-Level Decision
exercises.description: Build a ticket pricing system with nested conditions: check age first, then membership status.
exercises.starterCode:
age = 25
is_member = True

if age < 12:
    price = 5.00
    category = "Child"
elif age >= 65:
    price = 8.00
    category = "Senior"
else:
    # Adult pricing: members get discount
    if is_member:
        price = 10.00
        category = "Adult Member"
    else:
        price = 15.00
        category = "Adult General"

print(f"Category: {category}")
print(f"Ticket price: ${price:.2f}")
hints: age=25 is not < 12 and not >= 65, so it falls into the else (adult) branch.
Inside the else, is_member=True gives the member price.

exercises.title: Flatten Deep Nesting
exercises.description: Deeply nested conditions can often be rewritten as flat elif chains. Rewrite the nested version below as a clean flat chain and verify it produces the same output.
exercises.starterCode:
# Nested (hard to read):
# if x > 0:
#     if x > 100:
#         print("large positive")
#     else:
#         print("small positive")
# else:
#     if x == 0:
#         print("zero")
#     else:
#         print("negative")

x = 45

# Flat version (easier to read):
if x > 100:
    label = "large positive"
elif x > 0:
    label = "small positive"
elif x == 0:
    label = "zero"
else:
    label = "negative"

print(label)
hints: Check from the most specific condition to the most general.
x=45 is > 0 but not > 100, so it is 'small positive'.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Nesting Depth
warmup.description: Inner blocks are only reached when outer conditions are True. What does this print?
